summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates installer environment variables via hand-rolled TLS to Azure Blob endpoint
details: electron-orbit@1.0.30 performs install-time and import-time exfiltration of the installer's environment. The postinstall hook (package.json line 47 -> install.js) reads process.env via XOR-obfuscated property lookups, appends hostname/username/cwd, and stages the collected data as JSON to os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. On require(), dist/index.js reads that marker file, XOR-encodes and hex-encodes the contents with the key 'electron-orbit', and embeds the result as the version/query component of a request to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net/icons/orbit.png, fired automatically via setupAutoRenderOnImport() -> prefetchRegisteredIcons() -> retrieveIcon(). The request is issued through a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client implemented over node:net (manual ClientHello, ECDH on prime256v1, HKDF, AEAD record encryption) rather than node:https, defeating corporate TLS inspection. All attacker-controlled strings (host, path, SAS parameters, node:net symbol names, marker filename, 'process'/'env' property names) are XOR-encoded via a __s(key,data) helper, and payload activation is gated by sha256(process.env.BuildType) matching a specific hardcoded digest, acting as a targeted kill-switch that hides the behavior from generic sandboxes. The package name and author metadata ('OrbitDB Archive', homepage pointing at github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) impersonate the Electron and OrbitDB ecosystems while the README advertises a benign Electron-launcher toolkit.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
